California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Patrick Carroll Shea 
Lillick & McHose 
101 West Broadway, 18th Floor 
San Diego, CA 
92101 
Dear Mr. Shea: 
March 2, 1989 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-89-105 
You have requested advice concerning the duties of San Diego 
County Supervisor Susan Golding under the campaign provisions of 
the Political Reform Act (the "Act,,).l/ This letter confirms that 
your letter dated February 9, 1989, accurately summarizes the 
telephone advice I provided to you on that date. 
Please contact me at (916) 322-5901 if you have any further 
questions regarding this matter.

Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel

CO NflDrNTJAL 
1984 Campaign Deficiencies/Funds 
on 
Deposit 
from 
1988 campaign 
Dear Susan: 
Effect of Proposition 73 
Our File No. GOL12-001 
You 
have 
asked 
me to advise you regarding the ability 
of your campaign committee, Citizens for Susan 
Golding, 
to 
use 
contributions 
raised 
in the successful primary election of 1988 
to payoff the deficit of the 1984 campaign. 
This 
question 
has 
surfaced 
as 
a 
result 
of 
the 
enactment 
of last year's 
Proposition 73 
which 
restricts 
the 
use 
of 
campaign 
contributions 
to 
the 
specific campaign 
identified 
to 
the 
contributor at the time the contribution is made. 
The 
enactment 
of Proposition 73 
provided 
for 
an 
effective date 
of 
January 1, 
1989. 
From and after that date, 
contributions made to an 
individual 
running 
for 
a 
particular 
office 
may 
only 
be 
used by that individual in connection with 
the 
campaign 
for 
the 
designated office 
and 
may 
not 
be 
transferred 
to other 
candidates, 
nor used for campaigns other 
than that announced at the time the contribution was 
made. 
You 
have 
a 
surplus 
of 
campaign contributions from your successful 
1988 primary campaign victory and you would 
like 
to 
use 
those 
funds 
to payoff the 
debt 
incurred 
by 
your 
committee 
in 
connection 
with 
the 
1984 
campaign. 
Since this transfer would 
occur in February of 1989, you are 
concerned 
about 
the effect 
of Proposition 73 on the transfer. 
The 
Fair 
Political 
Practices 
Commission, 
through 
Attorney Kathryn E. Donovan, has 
confirmed 
that 
the 
effective 
date 
of 
Proposition 73 
restricts 
only 
the 
use 
of 
those 
contributions 
received 
on 
and 
after 
January 1, 
1989. 
Therefore, 
according 
to 
the FPPC, you may use the 1988 surplus 
to payoff the 1984 deficit, provided the funds 
in 
the 
surplus 
account 
were 
solicited 
and 
received 
in 
accordance with then 
applicable County ordinances 
respecting 
campaign 
contributions 
to persons running for a supervisor's office . 
. ' . 


Honorable Susan Golding 
February 9, 1989 
page Two 
CONfiDfNTJAJ. 
As always, I send my best wishes. 
PCS:wl

Sincerely, 
LILLICK & McHOSE 
PATRICK CARROLL SHEA 
A Professional corporation
